QF.id: VII Introduction to Securities Broking Operations_47

question.text: When an equity option is exercised, STT is charged at:

answer.text: 0.125% on the intrinsic value, payable by the buyer of the option
0.1% on the premium, payable by the seller
0.02% on the notional value, payable by the buyer
No STT on exercise of options

explanation.text: **The Logic:** When an equity option is exercised, STT of 0.125% is charged on the intrinsic value, payable by the BUYER of the option. On regular sale of options, STT of 0.1% is charged on the premium, payable by the seller. **The Trap:** Exercise STT (0.125% on intrinsic value) can be much larger than sale STT (0.1% on premium). Traders often prefer to sell/square off options rather than exercise to save on STT. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series VIII covers option exercise STT implications in trading strategies. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Option STT trap: Selling option = 0.1% on premium (small). Exercise = 0.125% on intrinsic value (can be large). Example: Bought call at premium Rs. 5, strike 100, market at 120. Intrinsic = 20. Exercise STT = 0.125% × 20 = Rs. 0.025/share. Sale STT would be 0.1% of market premium. Always compare before deciding to exercise vs. sell.
